[Guid("55E13E7E-52BD-4145-9ADF-9CF8B776C346")]

Examples

Sample Snippet

Fields

The fields collection for this object class.

virtual IFields Fields { get; }

Returns

IFields

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>Each table in a database has an ordered collection of fields, there is always at least one field in a table. The ordered collection behaves like a list, so it is possible to access individual fields by a numbered position (or index) in the list.</P> <P>A <STRONG>Fields </STRONG>collection can also exist independently of a table, for example when creating an index on a table you will need to define which fields take part in the index. There are many interfaces which either require or return a <STRONG>Fields </STRONG>collection; these include <STRONG>IClass </STRONG>(therefore also <STRONG>IObjectClass </STRONG>and <STRONG>IFeatureClass</STRONG>), <STRONG>ICursor</STRONG>, <STRONG>IIndex</STRONG>, <STRONG>IFeatureClassDraw</STRONG>, <STRONG>IRowBuffer</STRONG>, <STRONG>ISimpleDataConverter</STRONG>, <STRONG>ITableSort </STRONG>and <STRONG>IValidate</STRONG>.</P>

The index of the field with the specified name.

virtual int FindField(string Name)

Name string

Returns

int

Indexes

The indexes collection for this object class.

virtual IIndexes Indexes { get; }

Returns

IIndexes

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>The <STRONG>Indexes </STRONG>object represents a collection of indexes on a table. Indexes are designed to improve access times to data. The <STRONG>Indexes </STRONG>collection object operates in a very similar way to the Fields collection object.</P>

useBuffering bool

Parameters

QueryFilter IQueryFilter
buffer IRowBuffer

Workspace

The workspace containing this dataset.

virtual IWorkspace Workspace { get; }

Returns

IWorkspace

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<p>A Workspace is a container of spatial and non-spatial datasets such as feature classes, raster datasets, and tables. It provides methods to instantiate existing datasets and to create new datasets. Workspaces are classified into types specified by the <b><a href="esriWorkspaceType.htm">esriWorkspaceType</a></b> enumerator: FileSystemWorkspace, LocalDatabaseWorkspace, and RemoteDatabaseWorkspace. Shapefiles and ArcGIS for Desktop Advanced workspaces are examples of FileSystemWorkspace. A personal geodatabase stored in an Access or a File Geodatabase is an example of a LocalDatabaseWorkspace. A geodatabase stored in an RDBMS such as Oracle, DB2, SQL Server, or Informix and accessed via ArcSDE is an example of a RemoteDatabaseWorkspace.</p>&nbsp;<p>A Workspace hands out a <b><a href="WorkspaceName.htm">WorkspaceName</a>&nbsp;</b>object as the value of its FullName property. For example, the WorkspaceName for a workspace can be persisted in a map document. An application can call the <b>Open()</b> method on the workspace name after loading it from persistent storage to connect to and get an object reference to the workspace.</p>

WriteFeature(IFeature) 

virtual void WriteFeature(IFeature Feature)

Parameters

Feature IFeature

WriteFeatures(ISet) 

virtual void WriteFeatures(ISet features)

Parameters

features ISet
